name: Family house Rvasi
description: Traditional stone house in the village of Rvasi on the NW shore of Skadar Lake, 8km from Rijeka Crnojevića and 1km from the lake, with vineyard views and a garden. Genuine tier-3 WOW stay — stone-house character, rural isolation and lake proximity are the appeal. Sleeps 5; on-route for the Skadar/Cetinje leg.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.96 from 24 reviews. "Stone house on Skadar Lake" category draws strong reviews: guests across nearby properties cite "magnificent lake view," hosts who offer boat tours on the lake, fresh garden produce, and "feeling like part of the family." Quieter than Virpazar — better for a peaceful rural night, slightly less convenient for kayak logistics.
cost: ~€82/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Ivan's holiday home
description: House in Godinje, a medieval wine village on Skadar Lake's south shore, ~4km from Virpazar. Godinje produces some of Montenegro's best indigenous wine (Vranac and Krstač) from vineyards worked continuously since the 10th century. BBQ, terrace, free parking; sleeps 6.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.78 from 83 reviews. Guests at Godinje accommodations consistently mention fresh produce from the garden, homemade wine/brandy offered by hosts, and a "slow, authentically Montenegrin" pace. At ★4.78 this is the lowest-rated Skadar-zone option — Feel the village (★4.9) beats it on quality. Best if specifically wanting the wine-village experience over proximity to kayaks.
cost: ~€80/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Holiday Home Darja
description: Well-positioned 2-bed cottage between Skadar Lake and Bar at lat 42.20, rated the highest of the Skadar-zone wishlist options. Ideal for the night between finishing lake activities and hitting the Budva/coast — south of Virpazar, reducing the next morning's Budva drive. Sleeps 5; ~€68/night for 4.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.93 from 56 reviews — the strongest rating of the Skadar-area wishlist entries. The SW Skadar/Bar location is less central for lake activities (further from Virpazar kayaks) but better for southbound routing toward Budva the next day.
cost: ~€68/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Mountain Lodge (Duži, Šavnik)
description: Remote wooden cabin in the Šavnik valley, ~35km south of Žabljak on the natural Durmitor→Ostrog road. Sleeps exactly 4 (no spare bed). Post-Durmitor night in genuine mountain isolation before descending to Ostrog — no backtracking needed. Near Nevidio Canyon for a potential half-day add-on.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★5.0 from 17 reviews — small sample, treat with caution. Šavnik area praised for remote wilderness setting and proximity to Nevidio Canyon. Price not retrieved — check availability carefully (may have minimum-night requirements or be fully booked for June). Fallback: stay in Žabljak or push straight to Ostrog area.
cost: price not retrieved — didn't surface in June searches (likely already booked or has a min-night that excludes short stays); check listing

name: Zen House Sarajevo (Hot Tub)
description: Mountain oasis property at Hadžići on the western edge of Sarajevo with a 40°C outdoor jacuzzi year-round, mountain views and a terrace. Sleeps exactly 4. On the western approach from Mostar — convenient if arriving from Bosnia; needs a ~25 min drive to Baščaršija. WOW tier if the group wants a rural mountain night rather than a city apartment.
communityNotes: Rated 10/10 from 13 external reviews (Booking.com). Year-round outdoor jacuzzi is genuinely distinctive. Location is at Hadžići — 10 miles from Sarajevo airport, on the western approach from Mostar. The hot tub and mountain-feel experience justify the price over downtown apartments.
cost: price not retrieved — didn't surface in June searches (likely already booked or has a min-night that excludes short stays); check listing

name: 4seasons Pale Jahorina Cottage
description: Forest cottage on the Jahorina mountain slopes above Pale, ~25 min drive from Sarajevo's Baščaršija, sleeping 4. Jahorina summer = hiking, mountain biking and clean mountain air. A genuine forested alternative to a downtown Sarajevo apartment — terrace, fireplace, privacy — if the group wants to sleep in the mountains and drive into Sarajevo for the evening.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.88 from 89 reviews. Summer context: the Jahorina/Pale area is praised for "highest ozone in Europe — air spa" setting and meadow/forest landscape. Good tier-2/low-tier-3 mountain stay — not a lake-view villa, but genuinely pleasant vs a central Sarajevo apartment.
cost: ~€76/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Panoramic Mountain Chalet (Pale)
description: Mountain chalet with hot tub, BBQ, panoramic terrace and fireplace near Pale/Jahorina, ~30 min east of Sarajevo, sleeping 6. One of the stronger tier-3 WOW candidates in Bosnia — the combination of mountain deck, panoramic view, hot tub and group capacity at ~€93/night makes it genuinely standout. Only works if taking the Sarajevo overnight.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.94 from 17 reviews. Nearby Jahorina properties at similar ratings draw praise for "breathtaking mountain views" and "peaceful forested escape near Sarajevo." The low review count (17) means check recent reviews for maintenance quality before booking.
cost: ~€93/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Vikendica IVA (Jahorinski Potok)
description: Highly-rated mountain cabin near Jahorinski Potok — 1km from Ravna Planina, 21km from Sarajevo, sleeping 6. At ★4.98 from 40 reviews it is the best-reviewed property in the Bosnia wishlist. Peaceful mountain air, meadow/forest views; the strongest budget alternative to a Sarajevo city centre when the group wants a mountain sleep.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.98 from 40 reviews — the highest-rated Bosnia wishlist property and a Top Guest Favourite. The Ravna Planina area is described as "highest ozone in Europe — air spa." At ~€64/night this undercuts the Panoramic Chalet (~€93) while holding a higher rating. Best Sarajevo-area tier-3 pick if hot tub is not the priority.
cost: ~€64/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: HaDaNa Good Oasis (Tihovići)
description: From the user's Airbnb wishlist (taste signal, not a final pick). Cabin ~30 km N of Sarajevo. Off-route unless doing a Sarajevo loop. Sleeps 4. CAPACITY: sleeps exactly 4 (no spare bed).
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.95 from 22 reviews. Sleeps 4.
cost: price not retrieved — didn't surface in June searches (likely already booked or has a min-night that excludes short stays); check listing

name: Kostela Stone House (Poplat)
description: From the user's Airbnb wishlist (taste signal, not a final pick). Proven stone house in the Stolac/Berkovići hinterland SE of Mostar (157 reviews!). ON the Mostar→Trebinje corridor. Sleeps 5.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.94 from 157 reviews. Sleeps 5. | Duplicate: same property as ba-villa-kostela-stone-house-poplat (Kostela Stone House, Poplat). Keep the villa-file entry.
cost: price not retrieved — didn't surface in June searches (likely already booked or has a min-night that excludes short stays); check listing

name: Mountain Camp Burrows (Izgori)
description: Cluster of 4 wooden bungalows at 1180m in the Gacko highlands (SE Bosnia), 4km from the Gacko-Foča highway and next to Mt Volujak (2236m). Farm-to-table meals (mountain milk, fresh meat, vegetables), hiking and fishing on-site. Roughly on the Sutjeska→Montenegro corridor — a wilderness night between Sutjeska NP and Šćepan Polje.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.86 from 98 reviews. Described as pristine mountain nature with fresh farm food. Practical note: the Gacko-Foča highway is the direct line from Sutjeska NP toward Šćepan Polje — ~30 min detour off the main road. Verify road conditions and bungalow capacity (max occupancy ~5). Contact directly for June availability.
cost: price not retrieved — didn't surface in June searches (likely already booked or has a min-night that excludes short stays); check listing

name: Diva Cottage (Varvara/Rama)
description: From the user's Airbnb wishlist (taste signal, not a final pick). Cottage by Rama Lake, ~40 km NW of Konjic. Marginal detour off the Mostar→Konjic line. Sleeps 5.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★5.0 from 10 reviews. Sleeps 5.
cost: ~€107/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Apartment City Center Mostar
description: Found alternative for the Mostar overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Value champion in central Mostar — ★4.94 from 271 reviews. Walkable to Old Bridge.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.94 from 271 reviews.
cost: ~€53/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: The Old Almond
description: Found alternative for the Mostar overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Budget pick right by the Old Town, under €50/night.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.81 from 81 reviews.
cost: ~€48/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Apartment Neretva
description: Found alternative for the Mostar overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Higher-end central option, excellent reviews.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.95 from 101 reviews. | Redundant: Apartment Neretva Mostar €76. Too expensive for a plain city apt; Nana's House (€40) and the Old Almond (€48) cover the cheap Mostar tier.
cost: ~€76/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Apartments S&S 1
description: Found alternative for the Konjic overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Solid central Konjic apartment for the rafting/Neretva night.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.9 from 30 reviews. | Redundant: S&S Apartments Mostar €66. Three middle-tier Mostar city apartments is too many — Nana's House (€40 whole house) is obviously better at lower price.
cost: ~€66/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Guest House Konjic
description: Found alternative for the Konjic overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Long-running, well-reviewed guest house in central Konjic.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.86 from 87 reviews. | Redundant: Guest House Konjic €76. Too expensive for a plain Konjic apartment when the lake-view studio is €46. Middle tier, no special character.
cost: ~€76/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: The Pearl Downtown Sarajevo
description: Found alternative for the Sarajevo overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Value champion in downtown Sarajevo, under €50.
communityNotes: Address: Prote Bakovića 2 — 100m from Sebilj fountain, squarely in the old town core. Sarajevo Airbnbs at this price tier "over-deliver on what the photos suggest." One traveler: "gorgeous balcony overlooking the hills... would cost 4–5× more in London." Possible tram noise if facing the main streets; Prote Bakovića is uphill from the bar-noise zone so likely quieter. Walkability: 215 restaurants, 143 attractions within 0.3 miles. War Childhood Museum, Gazi Husrev-bey Mosque, bazaar all <10 min. Best price-to-location ratio on the whole trip.
bestTime: Night 6 (Jun 21) — Sarajevo overnight. Book for Jun 21 (1 night), 4 guests: airbnb.com/rooms/1542024184506963047. Check-in after ~19:00 (long Day 6 drive from Mostar via Jablanica/Boračko Lake).
cost: ~€48/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Baščaršija Center Apartment
description: Found alternative for the Sarajevo overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). In the old bazaar quarter, well-reviewed.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.88 from 83 reviews. | Redundant: Bascarsija Center €62 city apartment. Pearl Downtown is €48, same zone, better value. Three Sarajevo city apartments is too many.
cost: ~€62/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Gaga City Center
description: Found alternative for the Trebinje overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Outstanding value (€35, ★5.0/48) in central Trebinje — ideal cheap base near Dubrovnik airport.
communityNotes: In old town center, likely same host as nearby "Apartman Gaga." Trebinje consistently called "most underrated stop in the Balkans" — "least busy, least touristy old town in the region." City is Republika Srpska: Cyrillic, Orthodox flavor, very different from Mostar. Hot in June (35°C+). Highlights: Tvrdoš Monastery winery 20min away, flat walkable stari grad. Half a day covers it — good transit night Dubrovnik↔Montenegro. Generic Trebinje apartment reviews cite "exceptional comfort, outstanding cleanliness, superb location." At €35 for 4, cheapest night on the trip.
bestTime: Book on Airbnb: airbnb.com/rooms/1165836464333612445 — check Jun 18 AND Jun 27 (both Trebinje nights). Cheapest night on the trip.
cost: ~€35/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: FoRest & Relax
description: Found alternative for the Trebinje overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Top-rated Trebinje apartment, great for the pickup or final night.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.97 from 37 reviews.
cost: ~€53/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: SANSSOUCI Apartment
description: Found alternative for the Trebinje overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Central, perfect 5.0 rating; a comfortable Trebinje base.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★5.0 from 28 reviews.
cost: ~€56/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Black Pine 2 Zabljak
description: Centrally located Žabljak apartment, part of the Black Pine property family, at 1456m altitude in town. Budget base for Durmitor: close to hiking trailheads, with free parking and mountain views from the balcony. Cheapest well-rated Airbnb option for the zone at ~€65/night for 4.
communityNotes: Airbnb ★4.84 from 25 reviews. Host tips for hikes, equipped kitchen, free parking. Common zone caveat: steep interior staircase with luggage. Good fallback when the camps or the Mountain Spark cabin are full.
cost: ~€65/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Bungalow Krstajić 2
description: Found alternative for the Zabljak overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Wooden bungalow, lots of reviews; classic Durmitor lodging.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.76 from 120 reviews. | Redundant: Bungalow Krstajić 2 Žabljak €70. Black Pine 2 (€65) covers the same cheap-apartment Žabljak tier at lower price. Zone has camp + €65 apt + WOW cabin.
cost: ~€70/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Feel the village (Virpazar)
description: Highly-rated village guesthouse apartment right in Virpazar, walking distance from the kayak rental shacks and the Skadar Lake canal. Host produces her own wine and liquors, cooks evening meals on request, and books boat tours — genuine village immersion. At ~€48/night for 4, the value champion of the Skadar zone.
communityNotes: Rated 9.2 on Booking, ★4.9/68 on Airbnb. Guests: "clean, huge, bright and beautiful — comfy beds, very well equipped, beautiful views of mountains and lake"; host "booked us a boat tour" and serves own-made wine. Virpazar's central position makes this the easiest Skadar base: kayaks 5 min walk, Pavlova Strana 25 min drive.
cost: ~€48/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Villa Semeder
description: Found alternative for the Skadar overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Very well-reviewed Skadar Lake villa near Virpazar.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.91 from 142 reviews.
cost: ~€70/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Victoria's place
description: Found alternative for the Kotor overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Cheapest well-rated option in pricey Kotor old-town area.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.86 from 93 reviews. | Redundant: Victoria's place Budva €70. Same middle-tier bracket as Sea Side — zone covered better by Masha 1 and Got it.
cost: ~€70/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Apartments Albomi
description: Found alternative for the Kotor overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Top-rated Kotor apartment; Kotor runs expensive (€70+ floor).
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.96 from 96 reviews. | Redundant: Albomi Budva apartments €88. Over-budget for a plain Budva apartment; no special character. Zone already covered.
cost: ~€88/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Masha 1
description: Found alternative for the Budva overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Value+quality champion in Budva (€64, ★4.97/140).
communityNotes: Address: 27 Obilaznica — bypass road adjacent to old town, ~1.6km / 20min walk to old town citadel. Cross-listed Booking.com 9.2/10 "Fabulous." 42m², 1BR, full kitchen, balcony, AC, washing machine, parking (perk for a car group). Mogren Beach 4–5 min walk. "Great location, walking distance to old town and beach." 140 reviews at ★4.97 is strong track record for reliability. Caveat: Budva old town is theme-park busy in June — budget for walk back uphill at night or Uber. Old Airbnb ID = listing has been live for years, consistent quality.
cost: ~€64/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)

name: Sea Side, A&B Apartments
description: Found alternative for the Budva overnight zone (better-value option vs the wishlist). Well-reviewed Budva apartment near the beach.
communityNotes: Airbnb guest rating ★4.94 from 63 reviews. | Redundant: Sea Side Budva €70 apartment. Masha 1 (€64, ★4.97/140) is cheaper and better rated for the same zone.
cost: ~€70/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates)
